title: SET_PROFILE
text: SET_PROFILE Specify the network connection settings the UHTTP component uses to connect Uniface client applications to network resources. UHTTP.SET_PROFILE( "profile_name" ) Settings Profile Option Values Description min_tls_version 1.1 | 1.2 (default) | 1.3 For example: min_tls_version=1.3 Copy CURLOPT_SSLVERSION Defines the minimum TLS version used in the communication. This is comparable to min_tls_version in the TLS settings. cipher_list See Ciphers Supported by the TLS Connector For example: cpr_lst=AES128-GCM-SHA256 Copy CURLOPT_SSL_CIPHER_LIST Defines the list of ciphers which can be used by the connection. This is comparable to cipher_list in the TLS settings. verify_server { yes | y | 1 } | { no | n | 0 } Default value is yes . For example: verify_server=yes Copy CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER This option determines whether curl verifies the authenticity of the peer's certificate. By default, verify the authenticity of the peer's certificate. This is comparable to verify_server in the TLS settings. verify_server=no is also comparable to SET_FLAGS(2) verify_url_name { yes | y | 1 } | { no | n | 0 } Default value is yes . For example: verify_url_name=no Copy CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST By default, verify the certificate's name against the host. Check the server claimed identity. The URL should have the same name as the server’s name in the certificate. verify_url_name is comparable to verify_server_name in the TLS profile, but verif_url_name is used only in the UHTTP driver to validate the server name in the URL against the server certificate. verify_url_name=no is also comparable to SET_FLAGS(1) ca_location See ca_location in TLS Connection Options For example, in Linux: ca_location=/etc/ssl/certs Copy CURLOPT_CAPATH This specifies the directory that contains Certificate Authority (CA) certificates ca_certificate See ca_certificate Note: Does not work if verify_server=no Default is "USYSDIR\ca-bundle.crt" For example: ca_cert=usys:servercert.crt Copy CURLOPT_CAINFO This is the path to Certificate Authority (CA) bundle http_followlocation { yes | y | 1 } | { no | n | 0 } Default value is yes . For example: http_followlocation=yes Copy CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION By default, Uniface is configured to follow any location specified in the header that the server sends as part of an HTTP header in a 3xx response. The Location: header can specify a relative or an absolute URL to follow. This option is used only in the UHTTP component. http_followlocation=yes is also comparable to SET_FLAGS(16) Description The SET_PROFILE function sets security and HTTP communication options that the UHTTP component uses for sending and receiving requests and responses. This is similar to SET_FLAGS except SET_PROFILE causes the UHTTP component to use the network profile in the NET_SETTINGS section of the application's assignment (.asn) file. The SET_PROFILE function is the preferred way of working with the UHTTP component, compared to using SET_FLAGS . There are additional options available in SET_PROFILE (such as support for cipher lists and protocol definitions), and it provides a central place to manage the options; namely, the NET_SETTINGS section of your .asn file. When SET_PROFILE is used in a UHTTP component, the SET_FLAGS settings are completely ignored. For example Explain this code trigger detail variables handle vUHTTP numeric vStatus string vURI, vHeaders, vContent, vResponse endvariables newinstance "UHTTP", vUHTTP vURI = "https://www.google.com" ;Create list of headers putitem/id vHeaders, "Content-Type", "application/xml; charset=UTF-8" putitem/id vHeaders, "Accept-Charset", "ISO-8859-1 ; q = 0.4, UTF-16BE; q=0.9" vUHTTP-> SET_PROFILE("http_profile") vStatus = vUHTTP->SEND(vURI, "GET", "", "", vHeaders, vContent, vResponse) putmess "vStatus=%%vStatus%%%" putmess "vContent=%%vContent%%%" putmess vResponse end Copy The SET_PROFILE("http_profile") statement references the NET_SETTINGS section of the application assignment (.asn) file, like this: Explain this code [NET_SETTINGS] http_profile=verify_url_name=no, min_tls_version=1.2, verify_server=no, http_followlocation=yes Copy Uniface reads the assignment (.asn) file and parses all of the settings in the file. During parsing the settings, Uniface displays an error in the transcript window when it encounters an unknown or invalid setting. This is a minimal validation check and it does not validate URL'S and paths, to avoid slow start up of the Uniface application. The process continues and default values in the case of an error. Related Links Assignment Files A Uniface assignment file contains one or more assignment settings, which determine the environment of a Uniface application and control aspects of its behavior. Assignment settings can determine the location of application resources and servers, set environment variables, define application logicals, and set Uniface system settings to influence runtime behavior. [NET_SETTINGS] Use the [NET_SETTINGS] section of the assignment file to define TLS connection profiles, and their required keys or certificates. These can be used to configure different TLS connector paths. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
